text.title: mob-programming Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: "Backseat-driver mode" — Driver types ahead of the Navigator, makes silent decisions.
antipattern.symptom: One person dominates; others tune out; rotation feels theatrical.
antipattern.root-cause: Strong-style rule not enforced; team culture rewards speed over inclusion.
antipattern.fix: Re-instate strong style explicitly: "the keyboard only types what was said aloud". A facilitator names the violation in real time.

antipattern.description: No rotation timer; same Driver for an hour.
antipattern.symptom: Mob degenerates into pair + spectators; people leave the room mentally.
antipattern.root-cause: Timer not visible or not enforced.
antipattern.fix: Use templates/mob-session.sh or an app-based timer with an audible chime; rotate the moment it fires.

antipattern.description: Skipping the retrospective at session end.
antipattern.symptom: Same mistakes recur next mob (rotation slipped, parking lot ignored); no learning trail.
antipattern.root-cause: "We don't have time" — but skipping retro costs more than it saves.
antipattern.fix: Reserve last 10 minutes of every session for retro. Three prompts: what worked / what slowed us / one experiment.
